purpose: Adversarial clean-room verification of wpc_sound_horizon.json (in-model DNW13 bare sound horizon).
checks: R(z_drag) bare=0.53230 vs gam0-cancelled=0.53230 FRAME-INVARIANT OK
z_eq_bare=4368 vs z_eq_LCDM=3418: bare equality is EARLIER (higher z)
DECOMP contributions (Mpc): low-matter=+29.5  eta-baryon=+3.1  radiation-gamma^-4-suppression=+65.2 (INFLATES)  x_dec-bare-decoupling-limit=-45.5 (COMPENSATES)  NET-gamma-frame=+19.7
low-bare-matter is dominant NET driver? True (matter +29.5 vs net-gamma +19.7 vs baryon +3.1)
H0(bare) from harness alpha: with 147.09=52.931  with in-model r_d=39.014
NOTE harness bao_cmb_chi2 alpha (tracker grid)=38.506; probe uses modelV V alpha=37.888 (5-node forced) -- distinct fits, expected
discrepancies: SUMMARY-CAVEAT WORDING: bare z_eq (4368) > LCDM (3421) so equality is EARLIER, not 'delayed' as one summary caveat states; the JSON bare_parameters note ('earlier matter-radiation equality') is correct. r_s inflation is driven by lower H throughout (both om_m and om_r down), not by delayed equality. Mechanism conclusion unaffected.

MECHANISM-WORDING ERROR: the JSON bare_parameters note and the summary caveat say the 'gamma_bar0^-4 radiation suppression partly compensates/offsets'. The physically-consistent decomposition shows the OPPOSITE SIGN: radiation suppression alone INFLATES r_d by +65.2 Mpc (it is the single largest sub-effect). The genuine partial COMPENSATOR is the x_dec=1/(gam0(1+z_dec)) bare-decoupling limit (-45.5 Mpc), a distinct gamma effect. NET gamma-frame = +19.7 Mpc (mild inflation). Low bare matter (+29.5) remains the dominant NET driver, so the headline mechanism and the +35.7% number are UNAFFECTED; only the causal attribution of the gamma terms is stated with the wrong sign.

equation_number_verification.source_checked: ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/abs/1306.3208 (via WebFetch small-model read; partial content, treat as indicative not exhaustive)
equation_number_verification.Eq31_sound_horizon: CONFIRMED -- source: Eq (31) defines D_bar_s 'the volume-average sound horizon scale at any epoch'. Matches probe.
equation_number_verification.Eq49_drag_depth: CONFIRMED -- source: c*tau_d ~= 1 assigned to Eq (49) in Appendix B. Matches probe.
equation_number_verification.Eq22_lapse_gamma_bar0: CONFIRMED as the lapse equation -- source: Eq (22) gives the general gamma_bar0 relation; the tracker simplification (2+fv0)/2 is DERIVED, not verbatim. Probe label OK.
equation_number_verification.Eq20_21_tracker_OmM0_Omk0: PLAUSIBLE-NOT-VERBATIM -- source places the bare density parameters (Om_M,Om_R,Om_k,Om_Q) in Eqs (18)-(21); the specific OmM0/Omk0 tracker forms are within that block but the exact sub-numbering (20-21) is not verbatim confirmable.
equation_number_verification.Eq10_bare_Friedmann_with_radiation: NOT CONFIRMED -- source's read attributes the radiation Friedmann (first Buchert eq with Om_M+Om_R) to Eq (2), with density-parameter forms in (18)-(21); 'Eq 10' specifically could not be located and may be a mislabel. The FORM (H_bar^2=H_bar0^2[OmM0 x^-3+OmR0 x^-4], drop Q&curvature as fv->0) is standard and numerically validated regardless.
equation_number_verification.quoted_params_confirmed: eta_Bgamma=(5.1+/-0.5)e-10 CONFIRMED; g_*=3.36 CONFIRMED. Omega_B0_bar/T0/z_dec vary across the paper's numerical solutions (no single universal value in the source read); probe's DNW13 self-check reproduces Omega_B0_bar=0.0303 numerically.
equation_number_verification.verdict: FORMS all standard & numerically validated (LCDM gate passes, DNW13 self-check reproduces 0.0303, R frame-invariant). Eq 31 & Eq 49 labels CONFIRMED; Eq 22 confirmed as lapse; Eq 10 and Eq 20-21 labels NOT verbatim-confirmable (Eq 10 likely should be Eq 2). Citation-precision issue only; does not affect any number.
